summary: Data-only backfill verdict (did NOT open risk_rules). (a) PASS: should_fire/must_not_fire/severities/max_emitted/beta are byte-identical to the prior label.json — the backfill did not re-judge what fires. (b) FAIL: the MEASURED on-chain Chainlink FDUSD/USD series (0.99830 @22181387, 0.98240 @22183475, 0.99460 @22187054) is materially inconsistent with EVENT.md, which documents a ~$0.87 trough (~13% depeg). Measured max depeg is only ~1.76%, just ~0.17% at the documented trough block, and the 'recovery' block (0.9824) reads LOWER than the 'trough' block (0.9983) — a non-monotonic trajectory that contradicts the trough->recovery event shape. The swapped values also break each case's own premise: the trough note justifies DEPEG CRITICAL by 'price below the 0.91 LT', but 0.9983 is far above 0.91, so the should_fire CRITICAL is no longer supported by the measured data; the benign note says 'within ~4 bps' but the measured value is ~54 bps off. Root cause: ~94% of FDUSD trades on Binance; the secondary-market confidence run is NOT visible in an on-chain Ethereum Chainlink feed (the snapshot invariant itself says the depeg price is 'injected per-case'). (c) fidelity marks all 6 inputs measured=true and they reconcile with the draft prices, but this faithful-capture claim is itself wrong: an Ethereum on-chain feed cannot reproduce the off-chain depeg, so the honest treatment is to keep the injected secondary prices marked measured=false (correctly degraded), not to swap in a measured series that erases the event. REJECT: do not ratify.
